_description: Rules applied per question for France vs Morocco 2026-07-09 (Quarterfinal). REVISED after fetching live Smarkets sharp-market data (event 45178292, fetch_fra_mar_smarkets.py). CORRECTED: an earlier version of this note claimed the JTC crowd consensus was confirmed unformed via web/markets 'current_value'. That claim was wrong -- current_value is a binary open(50)/settled(0 or 100) flag, not a probability, verified by checking 209 traded+settled markets elsewhere in the event and finding only {0,50,100} ever appear. The correct, pre-existing project finding (data/crowd_consensus_prediction_research.md) is that no API access to the pre-close crowd consensus exists at all -- we simply cannot observe it, formed or not. RULE1's crowd+market average is therefore unavailable for a structural reason, not because trading is absent; RULE2 (submit at/near a verified sharp market) governs 12 of the 15 questions on its own merits.

match_context.jtc_crowd_status: not observable pre-close via any known API endpoint (structural platform limitation, not an empirical 'unformed' finding -- see corrected _description)

per_question.note: Smarkets Anytime goalscorer, Mbappe: bid=0.50/offer=0.5051, mid=0.5026 (liquid). Close to the pre-market estimate (0.54, built from his StatsBomb career rate blended down for the 2022 h2h suppression precedent). Nudged only slightly above the market (0.52) rather than fully adopting it, since the specific opponent-suppression regime check is a real, additional data point a generic market-maker may underweight -- kept the departure small per STRATEGIC_MARGIN_PUSH_RESEARCH.md discipline.

Smarkets Player SOT O/U0.5, Hakimi: bid=0.3378/offer=0.3876, mid=0.3627 (liquid). Blended with the pre-market StatsBomb-history estimate (0.30, from 0 SOT across 10 tracked WC appearances) -- the market is liquid and likely already reflects his 2026 attacking uptick, so weighted toward it rather than the career-history floor alone.

Smarkets Player SOT O/U0.5, Brahim Diaz: offer-only=0.4348 (illiquid, no bid), x0.945=0.4109. This is well above the pre-market personal-production estimate (0.25, from 1 SOT in 5 tournament games). Given this exact suppression pattern for this exact player already won +44.06 RBP this campaign (CAN-MOR) against an equally generous illiquid market, weighted the personal-zero evidence more heavily than the thin market quote, but moved up from 0.25 to 0.32 to avoid fully discounting the market's information content -- a partial, not full, override.

Smarkets Player SOT O/U1.5, Dembele: bid=0.2222/offer=0.2667, mid=0.24445 (liquid). The market's own number is already below the pre-market bimodal-pattern estimate (0.30) -- the market has independently arrived at a similar 'expect suppression against a tough defense' conclusion. Set close to market (0.27), consistent with the validated FRA-PAR precedent where trusting the market/empirical blend over a flatter reading won +31.83 RBP.

Smarkets Morocco SOT O/U3.5: Over(>=4) mid=0.4365. Solving for the implied Poisson lambda (3.377) and evaluating P(>=3) analytically gives 0.656 -- strikingly close to the pre-market empirical-floor estimate's own implied lambda (~3.33 from competitive-game average). Given this convergence between an independent market and the team's own empirical record, blended to 0.63, slightly below the pure market-implied number, retaining a small margin of the caution against team-SOT-ceiling projection that this campaign's worst loss (CAN-MOR, -80.83 RBP) was built on -- but the convergence here meaningfully de-risks this call relative to that prior failure, which had no market to check against at all.

Smarkets OU2.5 goals: Under 2.5 (<=2) mid=0.5362 (liquid). Averaged with the pre-market Poisson/Dixon-Coles/NB estimate (0.60) per RULE1's blending logic (market + model, crowd unavailable) -> 0.57, leaning toward the DIRECT market since it is this campaign's most reliable tier.

Smarkets Cards O/U4.5: Over 4.5 (5+) mid=0.2266 (bid=0.1754/offer=0.2778, real two-sided market). Well below the pre-market disciplinary-record-based estimate (0.32, which had already tried to price in an elimination-stakes uplift over the very low historical card rate). Weighted mostly toward the market (0.26) since it is a real, liquid two-sided quote and a DIRECT hit for exactly this question -- the elimination-stakes uplift concern is retained only as a small nudge above the raw market mid, not as the dominant factor.
Smarkets HT result: Draw mid=0.43865 (bid=0.4348/offer=0.4425, liquid). Essentially identical to the pre-market base-rate estimate (0.45); adopted directly as DIRECT with only trivial rounding.

No Smarkets market exists for this exact question (checked the full 173-market list for the event; no 'same goals both halves' or equivalent line). Unchanged from the pre-market estimate, cross-checked for consistency against the now-updated Q6 (0.57) and Q8 (0.44).

Smarkets Penalty to be awarded: No mid=0.7221 (Yes side has no posted bid/offer; implied Yes = 1-0.7221 = 0.2779 by complementarity). Smarkets Any player to be sent off: Yes mid=0.1472 (liquid). Combined via inclusion-exclusion assuming rough independence: 1-(1-0.2779)(1-0.1472)=0.3842. This is almost exactly the pre-market tournament-wide-empirical-rate estimate (0.38) -- kept at 0.38, now market-corroborated rather than base-rate-only.

No dedicated Smarkets market found for a substitute scoring or assisting (checked the full market list; no such line exists). Unchanged from the pre-market base-rate-formula estimate, validated in the most recent prior match (FRA-PAR, priced 0.33, actual NO).

Smarkets France corners O/U6.5: Over(>=7) mid=0.4277 (liquid). Solving for the implied Poisson lambda (6.21) and evaluating P(>=5) analytically gives 0.742 -- nearly identical to the pre-market empirical-trend estimate (0.74, from the 6/4/5/9 corners log and the confirmed 6+ hit in FRA-PAR). Kept at 0.74, now market-confirmed rather than empirical-only, which meaningfully reduces the risk this question was facing as a repeat of the CAN-MOR team-ceiling-projection failure.

No Smarkets market exists for a hydration-break-relative timing question (checked the full market list; only fixed time brackets like '1-10 minutes' exist, not hydration-break-relative windows). Unchanged -- still the confirmed worst-performing question category in the campaign, shaded flat to 0.50 regardless of any raw model output.
No Smarkets market exists for a stoppage-time-specific card question. Unchanged from the pre-market estimate for the same reason as Q13.

Smarkets FTR: France bid=0.6061/offer=0.6098, mid=0.60795 (liquid, tight spread). This is notably higher than the pre-market ordered-logit estimate (raw 0.5485, shaded to 0.52 for a qualitative Morocco-resilience adjustment). Per RULE1 (average crowd+market; crowd still unavailable) and RULE2 (DIRECT liquid market takes priority when no crowd exists), adopted the market value directly rather than retaining the qualitative shade -- the market already prices in everything the ordered logit captures plus real money's assessment of Morocco's tournament run, and DIRECT liquid FTR markets are this campaign's single best-performing tier (73.5% beat-crowd historically). The qualitative downward adjustment applied pre-market is retired now that a real anchor exists.
